---
title: "Offset Headings"
subtitle: "Quarto Extension"
author:
  - name: "Mickaël CANOUIL, _Ph.D._"
    orcid: "0000-0002-3396-4549"
    url: "https://mickael.canouil.fr"
format:
  html:
    output-file: index
    embed-resources: true
  typst:
    output-file: example-typst
    papersize: a4
    margin:
      x: 2.5cm
      y: 2.5cm
format-links:
  - html
  - typst
number-sections: true
filters:
  - offset-headings
---

## Untouched Heading

This heading has no attribute, so it keeps its level and its section number whether or not the filter runs.

## Shifted Heading {offset-headings-by="1"}

This level-2 heading becomes a level-3 heading; the surrounding headings keep their original levels.

## Recursive Cascade {offset-headings-by="1"}

Cascading is on by default, so this heading and its nested headings all shift by 1.

### Nested Heading

This level-3 heading becomes a level-4 heading.

#### Deeper Heading

This level-4 heading becomes a level-5 heading.

## Pull a Subsection Up

### Pulled-up Subsection {offset-headings-by="-1"}

This level-3 heading becomes a level-2 heading, joining the top level instead of nesting.

## Non-recursive Shift {offset-headings-by="1" offset-headings-recursive="false"}

This level-2 heading becomes a level-3 heading, but its nested heading is left untouched.

### Untouched Nested Heading

This level-3 heading keeps its original level.

## Capped Shift {offset-headings-by="3" offset-headings-max-level="4"}

A `by` of 3 would push this level-2 heading to level 5, but `max-level` of 4 caps it at level 4.

### Capped Nested Heading

This level-3 heading would become level 6 through the cascade, but the cap holds it at level 4 as well.

## Bounded Cascade {offset-headings-by="1" offset-headings-depth="1"}

This level-2 heading becomes a level-3 heading, and the cascade reaches only one level of descendants.

### Cascaded Nested Heading

This level-3 heading is within the cascade depth, so it becomes a level-4 heading.

#### Beyond the Cascade Depth

This level-4 heading is beyond the cascade depth limit, so it keeps its original level.
